generatedSentence: A newcomer named Johnny, wearing a somewhat whiffy pullover and a slightly kinked bowtie, blinked as he stood in a steamy doorway. The air was thick with the scent of ancient cypress. He’d received a cryptic invitation to a remarkable game of roulette, promising an escape from his ordinary life. He felt a mild worry but his ambition trumps caution, knowing this might be his only entrance. His nimble niece, surprisingly kempt for a hacker, had informed him about the city's dangerous outlaws. The game was held in a brick alleyway, a deathtrap disguised with gaudy decoration. Veejays projected onscreen images of jumping figures, a mimic of his own kinesis. A greedy figure, known for carjacking and killing, smirked across the plate, pitching a joke about farts. He had legitimatized his position through vicious acts. Johnny's privacy was compromised, his forehead furrowed. He sensed an impending counterattack, perhaps from outlaw lawyers who sought to vilify him. The drain gurgled, a mystery unfolding. The whole framework felt unjust. Suddenly, fireworks exploded, a decoy for the sneaking figures with blade and artillery. Johnny, displaying unexpected kungfu backbone and nimble speed, reacted to the impulse. He’d been favored by intelligence agents for this very moment. He saw a killjoy aiming for his kneecaps, but Johnny’s quick getaway was approximative, almost eastward. He felt a surge of gigantism, an unifying kinesis that allowed him to jiggle the assailant’s knobble, causing them to drop their weapon. The leakage from a burst pipe made the ground squiffy. A cranky outlaw then tried dirty counterattacks. With a final, generous giving of effort, Johnny shackled the last assailant, a whimsical act of justice. He wouldn't let them glorify their past transgressions. He saw the shimmering gems spilled from a garbage bin, reflecting the chalked pathway. The jerky movements of the defeated revealed their true weakness. He decided to rejoin his niece, his mind clear of the worry. The old trend of crime was broken. He watched the ketchup-like blood randomize on the ground, a grotesque format of the fight. This victory would entail a new era, leaving the vicious past behind.

He’d received a cryptic invitation to a remarkable game of roulette, promising an escape from his ordinary life. He felt a mild worry but his ambition trumps caution, knowing this might be his only entrance. His nimble niece, surprisingly kempt for a hacker, had informed him about the city's dangerous outlaws.

The game was held in a brick alleyway, a deathtrap disguised with gaudy decoration. Veejays projected onscreen images of jumping figures, a mimic of his own kinesis. A greedy figure, known for carjacking and killing, smirked across the plate, pitching a joke about farts. He had legitimatized his position through vicious acts. Johnny's privacy was compromised, his forehead furrowed. He sensed an impending counterattack, perhaps from outlaw lawyers who sought to vilify him. The drain gurgled, a mystery unfolding. The whole framework felt unjust.

Suddenly, fireworks exploded, a decoy for the sneaking figures with blade and artillery. Johnny, displaying unexpected kungfu backbone and nimble speed, reacted to the impulse. He’d been favored by intelligence agents for this very moment. He saw a killjoy aiming for his kneecaps, but Johnny’s quick getaway was approximative, almost eastward. He felt a surge of gigantism, an unifying kinesis that allowed him to jiggle the assailant’s knobble, causing them to drop their weapon. The leakage from a burst pipe made the ground squiffy. A cranky outlaw then tried dirty counterattacks.

With a final, generous giving of effort, Johnny shackled the last assailant, a whimsical act of justice. He wouldn't let them glorify their past transgressions. He saw the shimmering gems spilled from a garbage bin, reflecting the chalked pathway. The jerky movements of the defeated revealed their true weakness. He decided to rejoin his niece, his mind clear of the worry. The old trend of crime was broken. He watched the ketchup-like blood randomize on the ground, a grotesque format of the fight. This victory would entail a new era, leaving the vicious past behind.
rawPanelSentences: A newcomer named Johnny, wearing a somewhat whiffy pullover and a slightly kinked bowtie, blinked as he stood in a steamy doorway. The air was thick with the scent of ancient cypress. He’d received a cryptic invitation to a remarkable game of roulette, promising an escape from his ordinary life. He felt a mild worry but his ambition trumps caution, knowing this might be his only entrance. His nimble niece, surprisingly kempt for a hacker, had informed him about the city's dangerous outlaws.

The game was held in a brick alleyway, a deathtrap disguised with gaudy decoration. Veejays projected onscreen images of jumping figures, a mimic of his own kinesis. A greedy figure, known for carjacking and killing, smirked across the plate, pitching a joke about farts. He had legitimatized his position through vicious acts. Johnny's privacy was compromised, his forehead furrowed. He sensed an impending counterattack, perhaps from outlaw lawyers who sought to vilify him. The drain gurgled, a mystery unfolding. The whole framework felt unjust.

Suddenly, fireworks exploded, a decoy for the sneaking figures with blade and artillery. Johnny, displaying unexpected kungfu backbone and nimble speed, reacted to the impulse. He’d been favored by intelligence agents for this very moment. He saw a killjoy aiming for his kneecaps, but Johnny’s quick getaway was approximative, almost eastward. He felt a surge of gigantism, an unifying kinesis that allowed him to jiggle the assailant’s knobble, causing them to drop their weapon. The leakage from a burst pipe made the ground squiffy. A cranky outlaw then tried dirty counterattacks.

With a final, generous giving of effort, Johnny shackled the last assailant, a whimsical act of justice. He wouldn't let them glorify their past transgressions. He saw the shimmering gems spilled from a garbage bin, reflecting the chalked pathway. The jerky movements of the defeated revealed their true weakness. He decided to rejoin his niece, his mind clear of the worry. The old trend of crime was broken. He watched the ketchup-like blood randomize on the ground, a grotesque format of the fight. This victory would entail a new era, leaving the vicious past behind.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A determined young man with short, spiky black hair and a small, gold-rimmed monocle. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Johnny, a determined young man, stands in a steamy doorway, his brow furrowed as he receives a glowing, holographic invitation. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A greedy, hulking figure in a distorted mirror image smirks from behind a brick wall, pitching a joke across a reflective plate towards Johnny, whose forehead is tense. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Johnny, mid-kungfu stance, deftly uses kinesis to jiggle the knobble of a sneaking assailant holding a futuristic blade, while fireworks burst in the background. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Johnny, victorious and panting slightly, shackles a defeated figure next to a garbage bin spilling glowing gems, observing the ketchup-like blood on a chalked pathway, as a new dawn breaks.

definitions.definition: A professional person qualified (as by a law degree or bar exam) and authorized to practice law, i.e. represent parties in lawsuits or trials and give legal advice.
definitions.example: A lawyer's time and advice are his stock in trade. - aphorism often credited to Abraham Lincoln, but without attestation

definitions.definition: (by extension) A legal layman who argues points of law.
The burbot.
The stem of a bramble.
To practice law.
To perform, or attempt to perform, the work of a lawyer.
To make legalistic arguments.
To barrage (a person) with questions in order to get them to admit something.
definitions.example: You've been lawyered!

definitions.definition: A conduit allowing liquid to flow out of an otherwise contained volume; a plughole (UK)
definitions.example: The drain in the kitchen sink is clogged.

definitions.definition: An access point or conduit for rainwater that drains directly downstream in a (drainage) basin without going through sewers or water treatment in order to prevent or belay floods.
Something consuming resources and providing nothing in return.
definitions.example: That rental property is a drain on our finances.

definitions.definition: An act of urination.
One terminal of a field effect transistor (FET).
An outhole.
A drink.
To lose liquid.
definitions.example: The clogged sink drained slowly.

definitions.definition: To flow gradually.
definitions.example: The water of low ground drains off.

definitions.definition: To cause liquid to flow out of.
definitions.example: Please drain the sink. It's full of dirty water.

definitions.definition: To convert a perennially wet place into a dry one.
definitions.example: They had to drain the swampy land before the parking lot could be built.

definitions.definition: To deplete of energy or resources.
definitions.example: The stress of this job is really draining me.

definitions.definition: To draw off by degrees; to cause to flow gradually out or off; hence, to exhaust.
To filter.
To fall off the bottom of the playfield.
The passage of a door; a door-shaped entrance into a house or a room.
(crosswords, often in combination) A word that runs horizontally in the completed puzzle grid or its associated clue.
definitions.example: I solved all of the acrosses, but then got stuck on 3 down.

definitions.definition: From one side to the other.
definitions.example: she helped the blind man across;  the river is half a mile across

definitions.definition: On the other side.
definitions.example: If we sail off at noon, when will we be across?

definitions.definition: In a particular direction.
definitions.example: He leaned across for a book.

definitions.definition: (crosswords) Horizontally.
definitions.example: I got stuck on 4 across.

definitions.definition: To, toward or from the far side of (something that lies between two points of interest).
definitions.example: Fortunately, there was a bridge across the river.

definitions.definition: On the opposite side of (something that lies between two points of interest).
definitions.example: That store is across the street.

definitions.definition: Across from: on the opposite side, relative to something that lies between, from (a point of interest).
From one side to the other within (a space being traversed).
definitions.example: Could you slide that across the table to me, please?

definitions.definition: At or near the far end of (a space).
Spanning.
definitions.example: This poetry speaks across the centuries.

definitions.definition: Throughout.
definitions.example: All across the country, voters were communicating their representatives.

definitions.definition: So as to intersect or pass through or over at an angle.
definitions.example: Lay the top stick across the bottom one.

definitions.definition: In possession of full, up-to-date information about; abreast of.
The part of the face above the eyebrows and below the hairline.
Confidence; audacity
The upper part of a mobile phone, above the screen.
One who has recently come to a community; a recent arrival.
A new participant in some activity; a neophyte.
Worthy of being remarked or noted; notable
Uncommon; unusual

A game of chance, in which a small ball is made to move round rapidly on a circle divided off into numbered red and black spaces, the one on which it stops indicating the result of a variety of wagers permitted by the game.
A small toothed wheel used by engravers to roll over a plate in order to produce rows of dots.
A similar wheel used to roughen the surface of a plate, as in making alterations in a mezzotint.
The locus of a point on a plane curve that rolls without slipping along another fixed plane curve.
Any of the small incisions on a sheet of stamps, used as an alternative to perforations.
A cylindrical curler for the hair.
To separate or decorate by incisions made with a small toothed wheel.
definitions.example: to roulette a sheet of postage stamps

definitions.definition: To propel oneself rapidly upward, downward and/or in any horizontal direction such that momentum causes the body to become airborne.
definitions.example: Kangaroos are known for their ability to jump high.

definitions.definition: To cause oneself to leave an elevated location and fall downward.
definitions.example: She is going to jump from the diving board.

definitions.definition: To pass by a spring or leap; to overleap.
definitions.example: to jump a stream

definitions.definition: To employ a parachute to leave an aircraft or elevated location.
To react to a sudden, often unexpected, stimulus (such as a sharp prick or a loud sound) by jerking the body violently.
definitions.example: The sudden sharp sound made me jump.

definitions.definition: To increase sharply, to rise, to shoot up.
definitions.example: Share prices jumped by 10% after the company announced record profits.

definitions.definition: To employ a move in certain board games where one game piece is moved from one legal position to another passing over the position of another piece.
definitions.example: The player's knight jumped the opponent's bishop.

definitions.definition: To move to a position (in a queue/line) that is further forward.
definitions.example: I hate it when people jump the queue.

definitions.definition: To attack suddenly and violently.
definitions.example: The hoodlum jumped a woman in the alley.

definitions.definition: To engage in sexual intercourse with (a person).
To cause to jump.
definitions.example: The rider jumped the horse over the fence.

definitions.definition: To move the distance between two opposing subjects.
To increase the height of a tower crane by inserting a section at the base of the tower and jacking up everything above it.
To increase speed aggressively and without warning.
To expose to danger; to risk; to hazard.
(smithwork) To join by a buttweld.
To thicken or enlarge by endwise blows; to upset.
(quarrying) To bore with a jumper.
To coincide; to agree; to accord; to tally; followed by with.
To start executing code from a different location, rather than following the program counter.
To flee; to make one's escape.
The act of performing a jump.
Exuberantly active; in full swing.
The state of being secluded from the presence, sight, or knowledge of others.
definitions.example: I need my privacy, so please stay out of my room.

definitions.definition: Freedom from unwanted or undue disturbance of one private life.
definitions.example: It takes a village to rob one of a sense of privacy.

definitions.definition: Freedom from damaging publicity, public scrutiny, surveillance, and disclosure of personal information, usually by a government or a private organization.
definitions.example: Privacy is assumed by many to be among common-law rights.

definitions.definition: A place of seclusion.
A relationship between parties seen as being a result of their mutual interest or participation in a given transaction, contract etc.; Privity.
Secrecy.
A private matter; a secret.
A man's necktie tied in a bow around the throat.
A kind of road intersection. See Bowtie (road).
Having the shape that the displayed part of a bowtie does when the latter is tied.
definitions.example: The corkscrew pasta holds the cheese sauce better, but all I had in the cupboard was bowtie pasta.

definitions.definition: A hardened rectangular block of mud, clay etc., used for building.
definitions.example: This wall is made of bricks.

definitions.definition: Such hardened mud, clay, etc. considered collectively, as a building material.
definitions.example: This house is made of brick.

definitions.definition: Something shaped like a brick.
definitions.example: a plastic explosive brick

definitions.definition: A helpful and reliable person.
definitions.example: Thanks for helping me wash the car. You're a brick.

definitions.definition: A shot which misses, particularly one which bounces directly out of the basket because of a too-flat trajectory, as if the ball were a heavier object.
definitions.example: We can't win if we keep throwing up bricks from three-point land.

definitions.definition: A power brick; an external power supply consisting of a small box with an integral male power plug and an attached electric cord terminating in another power plug.
An electronic device, especially a heavy box-shaped one, that has become non-functional or obsolete.
A carton of 500 rimfire cartridges, which forms the approximate size and shape of a brick.
A community card (usually the turn or the river) which does not improve a player's hand.
definitions.example: The two of clubs was a complete brick on the river.

definitions.definition: The colour brick red.
One kilo of cocaine.
To build with bricks.
To make into bricks.
To hit someone or something with a brick.
To make an electronic device nonfunctional and usually beyond repair, essentially making it no more useful than a brick.
definitions.example: My VCR was bricked during the lightning storm.

definitions.definition: (of weather) Extremely cold.
Warm and humid; full of steam
definitions.example: My glasses fogged up when I walked into the steamy room.

definitions.definition: Resembling or characteristic of steam
Erotic
definitions.example: Her latest novel was very steamy, but still managed to top the charts.

definitions.definition: To creep or go stealthily; to come or go while trying to avoid detection, as a person who does not wish to be seen.
definitions.example: He decided to sneak into the kitchen for a second cookie while his mom was on the phone.

definitions.definition: To take something stealthily without permission.
definitions.example: I went to sneak a chocolate but my dad caught me.

definitions.definition: (ditransitive) To stealthily bring someone something.
definitions.example: She asked me to sneak her a phone next month.

definitions.definition: To hide, especially in a mean or cowardly manner.
(with on) To inform an authority of another's misdemeanours.
definitions.example: If you sneak on me I'll bash you!

definitions.definition: The act of one who sneaks.
Secret or underhand; not openly avowed.
Something secret or unexplainable; an unknown.
definitions.example: The truth behind the events remains a mystery.

definitions.definition: Someone or something with an obscure or puzzling nature.
definitions.example: That man is a mystery.

definitions.definition: A secret or mystical meaning.
A religious truth not understandable by the application of human reason alone (without divine aid).
(obsolete outside Eastern Orthodoxy) A sacrament.
(chiefly in plural) A secret religious celebration, admission to which was usually through initiation.
definitions.example: the Eleusinian mysteries

definitions.definition: A particular event or series of events in the life of Christ.
definitions.example: The second decade of the Rosary concerns the Sorrowful mysteries, such as the crucifixion and the crowning with thorns.

definitions.definition: A craft, art or trade; specifically a guild of craftsmen.
An assailant.
To urge with violence.
Involving extreme force or motion.
definitions.example: A violent wind ripped the branch from the tree.

definitions.definition: Involving physical conflict.
definitions.example: We would rather negotiate, but we will use violent means if necessary.

definitions.definition: Likely to use physical force.
definitions.example: The escaped prisoners are considered extremely violent.

definitions.definition: Intensely vivid.
definitions.example: The artist expressed his emotional theme through violent colors.

definitions.definition: Produced or effected by force; not spontaneous; unnatural.
The substance constituting earth's atmosphere, particularly:
definitions.example: I'm going outside to get some air.

definitions.definition: (usually with the) The apparently open space above the ground which this substance fills, formerly thought to be limited by the firmament but now considered to be surrounded by the near vacuum of outer space.
definitions.example: The flock of birds took to the air.

definitions.definition: A breeze; a gentle wind.
A feeling or sense.
definitions.example: to give it an air of artistry and sophistication

definitions.definition: A sense of poise, graciousness, or quality.
(usually in the plural) Pretension; snobbishness; pretence that one is better than others.
definitions.example: putting on airs

definitions.definition: A song, especially a solo; an aria.
Nothing; absence of anything.
An air conditioner or the processed air it produces.
definitions.example: Could you turn on the air?

definitions.definition: Any specific gas.
(motor sports) A jump in which one becomes airborne.
A television or radio signal.
Publicity.
To bring (something) into contact with the air, so as to freshen or dry it.
To let fresh air into a room or a building, to ventilate.
To discuss varying viewpoints on a given topic.
To broadcast (a television show etc.).
To be broadcast.
definitions.example: This game show first aired in the 1990s and is still going today.

definitions.definition: To ignore.
An emission of digestive gases from the anus; a flatus.
(impolite) An irritating person; a fool.
(impolite, potentially offensive) (usually as "old fart") An elderly person; especially one perceived to hold old-fashioned views.
(impolite) To emit digestive gases from the anus; to flatulate.
(usually as "fart around") To waste time with idle and inconsequential tasks; to go about one's activities in a lackadaisical manner; to be lazy or over-relaxed in one's manner or bearing.
To emit (fumes, gases, etc.).
To look upon fondly; to prefer.
To encourage, conduce to
To do a favor [noun sense 1] for; to show beneficence toward.
definitions.example: Would you favor us with a poetry reading?

definitions.definition: To treat with care.
definitions.example: Favoring your sore leg will only injure the other one.

definitions.definition: (including) To resemble, to look like (another person).
Treated or regarded with partiality.
(in combination) Having a certain appearance or physical features.
definitions.example: ill-favoured; well-favoured; hard-favoured

definitions.definition: Wearing a favour.
definitions.example: a white-favoured footman

definitions.definition: A slightly curved but almost flat dish from which food is served or eaten.
definitions.example: I filled my plate from the bountiful table.

definitions.definition: Such dishes collectively.
The contents of such a dish.
definitions.example: I ate a plate of beans.

definitions.definition: A course at a meal.
definitions.example: The meat plate was particularly tasty.

definitions.definition: An agenda of tasks, problems, or responsibilities
definitions.example: With revenues down and transfer payments up, the legislature has a full plate.

definitions.definition: A flat metallic object of uniform thickness.
definitions.example: A clutch usually has two plates.

definitions.definition: A vehicle license plate.
definitions.example: He stole a car and changed the plates as soon as he could.

definitions.definition: A taxi permit, especially of a metal disc.
A layer of a material on the surface of something, usually qualified by the type of the material; plating
definitions.example: The bullets just bounced off the steel plate on its hull.

definitions.definition: A material covered with such a layer.
definitions.example: If you're not careful, someone will sell you silverware that's really only silver plate.

definitions.definition: A decorative or food service item coated with silver or gold.
definitions.example: The tea was served in the plate.

definitions.definition: A weighted disk, usually of metal, with a hole in the center for use with a barbell, dumbbell, or exercise machine.
An engraved surface used to transfer an image to paper.
definitions.example: We finished making the plates this morning.

definitions.definition: An image or copy.
An illustration in a book, either black and white, or colour, usually on a page of paper of different quality from the text pages.
A shaped and fitted surface, usually ceramic or metal that fits into the mouth and in which teeth are implanted; a dental plate.
A horizontal framing member at the top or bottom of a group of vertical studs.
A foot, from "plates of meat".
definitions.example: Sit down and give your plates a rest.

definitions.definition: Home plate.
definitions.example: There was a close play at the plate.

definitions.definition: A tectonic plate.
Plate armour.
definitions.example: He was confronted by two knights in full plate.

definitions.definition: Any of various larger scales found in some reptiles.
A flat electrode such as can be found in an accumulator battery, or in an electrolysis tank.
The anode of a vacuum tube.
definitions.example: Regulating the oscillator plate voltage greatly improves the keying.

definitions.definition: Silver or gold, in the form of a coin, or less often silver or gold utensils or dishes (from Spanish plata ("silver")).
A roundel of silver or tinctured argent.
A prize given to the winner in a contest.
Any flat piece of material such as coated glass or plastic.
(travel industry) A metallic card, used to imprint tickets with an airline's logo, name, and numeric code.
(travel industry, by extension) The ability of a travel agent to issue tickets on behalf of a particular airline.
A VIN plate, particularly with regard to the car's year of manufacture.
One of the thin parts of the brisket of an animal.
A very light steel horseshoe for racehorses.
(furriers' slang) Skins for fur linings of garments, sewn together and roughly shaped, but not finally cut or fitted.
(hat-making) The fine nap (as of beaver, musquash, etc.) on a hat whose body is made from inferior material.
A record, usually vinyl.
To cover the surface material of an object with a thin coat of another material, usually a metal.
definitions.example: This ring is plated with a thin layer of gold.

definitions.definition: To place the various elements of a meal on the diner's plate prior to serving.
definitions.example: After preparation, the chef will plate the dish.

definitions.definition: To score a run.
definitions.example: The single plated the runner from second base.

definitions.definition: To arm or defend with metal plates.
To beat into thin plates.
(travel industry) To specify which airline a ticket will be issued on behalf of.
definitions.example: Tickets are normally plated on an itinerary's first international airline.

definitions.definition: To categorise stamps based on their position on the original sheet, in order to reconstruct an entire sheet.
(particularly with early British stamps) to identify the printing plate used.
To cover or smear with pitch.
To darken; to blacken; to obscure.
To throw.
definitions.example: He pitched the horseshoe.

definitions.definition: To throw (the ball) toward a batter at home plate.
To play baseball in the position of pitcher.
definitions.example: Bob pitches today.

definitions.definition: To throw away; discard.
definitions.example: He pitched the candy wrapper.

definitions.definition: To promote, advertise, or attempt to sell.
definitions.example: He pitched the idea for months with no takers.

definitions.definition: To deliver in a certain tone or style, or with a certain audience in mind.
definitions.example: At which level should I pitch my presentation?

definitions.definition: To assemble or erect (a tent).
definitions.example: Pitch the tent over there.

definitions.definition: To fix or place a tent or temporary habitation; to encamp.
To move so that the front of an aircraft or boat goes alternatively up and down.
To play a short, high, lofty shot that lands with backspin.
definitions.example: The only way to get on the green from here is to pitch the ball over the bunker.

definitions.definition: To bounce on the playing surface.
definitions.example: The ball pitched well short of the batsman.

definitions.definition: (of snow) To settle and build up, without melting.
To alight; to settle; to come to rest from flight.
(with on or upon) To fix one's choice.
To plunge or fall; especially, to fall forward; to decline or slope.
definitions.example: The field pitches toward the east.

definitions.definition: (of an embankment, roadway) To set, face, or pave with rubble or undressed stones.
(of a price, value) To set or fix.
(of a card) To discard for some gain.
To produce a note of a given pitch.
To fix or set the tone of.
The act of throwing or casting.
definitions.example: wild pitching in baseball

definitions.definition: The rough paving of a street to a grade with blocks of stone.
A facing of stone laid upon a bank to prevent wear by tides or currents.
To steal an automobile forcibly from (someone).
definitions.example: Someone should carjack that pompous jerk and teach him a lesson!

definitions.definition: To forcibly steal (a vehicle).
definitions.example: These twerps have carjacked their last Mercedes.

definitions.definition: The violent hijacking of a vehicle and sometimes its driver.
A precious stone, usually of substantial monetary value or prized for its beauty or shine.
Any precious or highly valued thing or person.
definitions.example: She's an absolute gem.

definitions.definition: Anything of small size, or expressed within brief limits, which is regarded as a gem on account of its beauty or value, such as a small picture, a verse of poetry, or an epigram.
definitions.example: a gem of wit

definitions.definition: A gemma or leaf-bud.
A type of geometrid moth, Orthonama obstipata.
A package containing programs or libraries for the Ruby programming language.
A size of type between brilliant (4-point) and diamond (4½-point), running 222 lines to the foot.
To adorn with, or as if with, gems.
Food waste material of any kind.
definitions.example: Garbage is collected on Tuesdays; rubbish on Fridays

definitions.definition: Useless or disposable material; waste material of any kind.
definitions.example: The garbage truck collects all residential municipal waste.

definitions.definition: A place or receptacle for waste material.
definitions.example: He threw the newspaper into the garbage.

definitions.definition: Nonsense; gibberish.
Something or someone worthless.
The bowels of an animal; refuse parts of flesh; offal.
To eviscerate
Bad, crap, shitty
definitions.example: He gave me some pretty garbage advice last night.

definitions.definition: Someone who presents a television programme of videos; a video jockey.
Vagina, vulva.
A person who is very old.
A person who lived in ancient times.
One of the senior members of the Inns of Court or of Chancery.
A senior; an elder; a predecessor.
Having lasted from a remote period; having been of long duration; of great age, very old.
definitions.example: an ancient city  an ancient forest

definitions.definition: Existent or occurring in time long past, usually in remote ages; belonging to or associated with antiquity; old, as opposed to modern.
definitions.example: an ancient author  an ancient empire

definitions.definition: (history) Relating to antiquity as a primarily European historical period; the time before the Middle Ages.
Experienced; versed.
Former; sometime.
Characterized by physical jerking.
Having the behavior of a jerk (unpleasant person).
An attack made in response to an attack by the opponents
To attack in response to an attack by opponents
Weak, unwell.
(of a machine, etc.) Not in good working condition.
Grouchy, grumpy, irritable; easily upset.
definitions.example: He got home from a long day at work tired and cranky.

definitions.definition: Not in perfect mental working order; eccentric, peculiar.
Full of spirit; spirited.
The sharp cutting edge of a knife, chisel, or other tool, a razor blade/sword.
The flat functional end of a propeller, oar, hockey stick, screwdriver, skate, etc.
The narrow leaf of a grass or cereal.
The thin, flat part of a plant leaf, attached to a stem (petiole). The lamina.
A flat bone, especially the shoulder blade.
A cut of beef from near the shoulder blade (part of the chuck).
The part of the tongue just behind the tip, used to make laminal consonants.
A sword or knife.
A piece of prepared, sharp-edged stone, often flint, at least twice as long as it is wide; a long flake of ground-edge stone or knapped vitreous stone.
(ultimate frisbee) A throw characterized by a tight parabolic trajectory due to a steep lateral attitude.
The rudder, daggerboard, or centerboard of a vessel.
A bulldozer or surface-grading machine with mechanically adjustable blade that is nominally perpendicular to the forward motion of the vehicle.
A dashing young man.
A homosexual, usually male.
Thin plate, foil.
One of a series of small plates that make up the aperture or the shutter of a camera.
(in the plural) The principal rafters of a roof.
The four large shell plates on the sides, and the five large ones of the middle, of the carapace of the sea turtle, which yield the best tortoise shell.
Airfoil in windmills and windturbines.
A blade server.
An exterior product of vectors. (The product may have more than two factors. Also, a scalar counts as a 0-blade, a vector as a 1-blade; an exterior product of k vectors may be called a k-blade.)
The part of a key that is inserted into the lock.
To skate on rollerblades.
definitions.example: Want to go blading with me later in the park?

definitions.definition: To furnish with a blade.
To put forth or have a blade.
To stab with a blade
definitions.example: The gang member got bladed in a fight.

definitions.definition: To cut (a person) so as to provoke bleeding.
A kind of piton used in thin cracks.
An act of leaking, or something that leaks
The amount lost due to a leak
An undesirable flow of electric current through insulation
Loss of retail stock, especially due to theft
(sound recording) The situation where sound is picked up by a microphone from a source other than that which is intended.
A tomato-vinegar-based sauce, sometimes containing spices, onion or garlic, and (especially in the US) sweeteners.
Such a sauce more generally (not necessarily based on tomatoes).
definitions.example: fish ketchup; fruit ketchup; mushroom ketchup

definitions.definition: To cover with ketchup.
The flat, roundish bone in the knee.
(roofing) A metal cover trim that fits over a panel rib after it has been cut and bent.
A cap or strong covering for the knees, used chiefly for horses, to protect their knees in case of a fall.
Cloth-covered frame used for protection against rain or sun.
definitions.example: Quick, grab that umbrella before you get rained on!

definitions.definition: Generally, anything that provides protection.
definitions.example: The fighters provide a defensive air umbrella over the battle group.

definitions.definition: Something that covers a wide range of concepts, purposes, groups, etc.
definitions.example: The test facility was established under the umbrella of the company's quality program.

definitions.definition: The main body of a jellyfish, excluding the tentacles.
definitions.example: Jellyfish are composed of more than 90% water and most of their umbrella mass is made up of gelatinous material.

definitions.definition: An umbrella-shaped reflector with a white or silvery inner surface, used to diffuse a nearby light.
To cover or protect, as if by an umbrella.
To form the dome shape of an open umbrella.
To move like a jellyfish.
(grammar) A grammatical construct that expresses approximation.
Of, relating to, or being an estimate or approximation.

An evergreen coniferous tree with flattened shoots bearing small scale-like leaves, whose dark foliage is sometimes associated with mourning, in family Cupressaceae, especially the genera Cupressus and Chamaecyparis.
To close and reopen both eyes quickly.
definitions.example: The loser in the staring game is the person who blinks first.

definitions.definition: To flash on and off at regular intervals.
definitions.example: The blinking text on the screen was distracting.

definitions.definition: To perform the smallest action that could solicit a response.
To shut out of sight; to evade; to shirk.
definitions.example: to blink the question

definitions.definition: To trick; to deceive.
To turn slightly sour, or blinky, as beer, milk, etc.
To teleport, mostly for short distances.
Affected with blinking.
A means of escape.
The effecting of an escape.
A vacation or holiday, or the destination for one.
Pertaining to an escape, as in a vehicle or plans.
definitions.example: They'd been discussing their getaway plans for weeks.

definitions.definition: The act of inviting; solicitation; the requesting of a person's company.
definitions.example: an invitation to a party, to a dinner, or to visit a friend

definitions.definition: A document or verbal message conveying an invitation.
definitions.example: We need to print off fifty invitations for the party.

definitions.definition: Allurement; enticement.
A line that is intentionally left open to encourage the opponent to attack.
The brief exhortation introducing the confession in the Anglican communion-office.
To make legitimate.
To legalize.
The act of adorning, embellishing, or honoring; ornamentation.
That which adorns, enriches, or beautifies; something added by way of embellishment; ornament.
Specifically, any mark of honor to be worn upon the person, as a medal, cross, or ribbon of an order of knighthood, bestowed for services in war, great achievements in literature, art, etc.
The use of exotic sugars as decoys to distract the immune system of a host
To put to death; to extinguish the life of.
definitions.example: Smoking kills more people each year than alcohol and drugs combined.

definitions.definition: To render inoperative.
definitions.example: He killed the engine and turned off the headlights, but remained in the car, waiting.

definitions.definition: To stop, cease or render void; to terminate.
definitions.example: My computer wouldn't respond until I killed some of the running processes.

definitions.definition: To amaze, exceed, stun or otherwise incapacitate.
definitions.example: That joke always kills me.

definitions.definition: To cause great pain, discomfort or distress to.
definitions.example: These tight shoes are killing my feet.

definitions.definition: To produce feelings of dissatisfaction or revulsion in.
definitions.example: It kills me to learn how many poor people are practically starving in this country while rich moguls spend such outrageous amounts on useless luxuries.

definitions.definition: To use up or to waste.
definitions.example: He told the bartender, pointing at the bottle of scotch he planned to consume, "Leave it, I'm going to kill the bottle."

definitions.definition: To exert an overwhelming effect on.
definitions.example: Between the two of us, we killed the rest of the case of beer.

definitions.definition: To overpower, overwhelm or defeat.
definitions.example: The team had absolutely killed their traditional rivals, and the local sports bars were raucous with celebrations.

definitions.definition: To force a company out of business.
To produce intense pain.
definitions.example: You don't ever want to get rabies. The doctor will have to give you multiple shots and they really kill.

definitions.definition: To punish severely.
definitions.example: My parents are going to kill me!

definitions.definition: To strike (a ball, etc.) with such force and placement as to make a shot that is impossible to defend against, usually winning a point.
To cause (a ball, etc.) to be out of play, resulting in a stoppage of gameplay.
To succeed with an audience, especially in comedy.
To cause to assume the value zero.
(IRC) To disconnect (a user) involuntarily from the network.
To deadmelt.
An instance of someone being killed.
(usually as make a killing) A large amount of money.
definitions.example: He made a killing on the stock market.

definitions.definition: That literally deprives of life; lethal, deadly, fatal.
Devastatingly attractive.
That makes one ‘die’ with laughter; very funny.
To say defamatory things about someone or something; to speak ill of.
To belittle through speech; to put down.

A device using gunpowder and other chemicals which, when lit, emits a combination of coloured flames, sparks, whistles or bangs, and sometimes made to rocket high into the sky before exploding, used for entertainment or celebration.
definitions.example: Commercial firework displays are commonly launched from boats or other vessels

definitions.definition: An event or a display where fireworks are set off.
A boisterous or violent event or situation.
definitions.example: I left the room after John came home drunk but before the fireworks went off.

definitions.definition: To shake something gently; to rattle or wiggle.
definitions.example: Jiggle the handle and see if the water stops.

definitions.definition: To shake, rattle, or wiggle.
definitions.example: The jelly jiggled in the bowl for a few moments after it was set down.

definitions.definition: A prostitute's client.
A device or place to urinate and defecate: now usually a toilet or lavatory, but also a chamber pot or outhouse.
A Western man traveling in East Asia.
A male mule.
A condom.
An inexperienced new worker, usually an immigrant.
A hospital gown: a gown with a back opening closed with snaps or ties, worn by hospital patients.

A device used to dampen the effects of such things as sound, light, or fluid. Specifically, a baffle is a surface which is placed inside an open area to inhibit direct motion from one part to another, without preventing motion altogether.
definitions.example: Tanker trucks use baffles to keep the liquids inside from sloshing around.

definitions.definition: An architectural feature designed to confuse enemies or make them vulnerable.
(coal mining) A lever for operating the throttle valve of a winding engine.
To publicly disgrace, especially of a recreant knight.
To hoodwink or deceive (someone).
To bewilder completely; to confuse or perplex.
definitions.example: I am baffled by the contradictions and omissions in the instructions.

definitions.definition: To foil; to thwart.
To struggle in vain.
definitions.example: A ship baffles with the winds.

definitions.definition: To exalt, or give glory or praise to (something or someone).
To make (something) appear to be more glorious than it is; regard something or someone as excellent baselessly.
To worship or extol.
Having greed; consumed by selfish desires.
Prone to overeat.
Tending to match as much text as possible.
definitions.example: This regular expression performs a greedy match.

definitions.definition: (of an algorithm) That tries to find the global optimum by finding the local optimum at each stage.
(ditransitive) To move, shift, provide something abstract or concrete to someone or something or somewhere.
(ditransitive) To estimate or predict (a duration or probability) for (something).
definitions.example: I give it a 95% chance of success.

definitions.definition: To yield slightly when a force is applied.
To collapse under pressure or force.
definitions.example: One pillar gave, then more, and suddenly the whole floor pancaked onto the floor below.

definitions.definition: To provide, as, a service or a broadcast.
definitions.example: They're giving my favorite show!

definitions.definition: To lead (onto or into).

definitions.definition: To provide a view of.
definitions.example: His window gave the park.

definitions.definition: To exhibit as a product or result; to produce; to yield.
definitions.example: The number of men, divided by the number of ships, gives four hundred to each ship.

definitions.definition: To cause; to make; used with the infinitive.
To cause (someone) to have; produce in (someone); effectuate.
To allow or admit by way of supposition; to concede.
definitions.example: He can be bad-tempered, I'll give you that, but he's a hard worker.

definitions.definition: To attribute; to assign; to adjudge.
To communicate or announce (advice, tidings, etc.); to pronounce or utter (an opinion, a judgment, a shout, etc.).

definitions.definition: To grant power or permission to; to allow.
To devote or apply (oneself).
definitions.example: That boy is given to fits of bad temper.

definitions.definition: To become soft or moist.
To shed tears; to weep.
To have a misgiving.
To be going on, to be occurring

definitions.definition: The act of bestowing as a gift; a conferring or imparting.
A gift; a benefaction.
The act of softening, breaking, or yielding.
Having the tendency to give; generous
One who is expert at programming and solving problems with a computer.
One who uses a computer to gain unauthorized access to data, or to carry out malicious attacks.
A computer security professional.
Something that hacks; a tool or device for hacking.
A fork-shaped tool used to harvest root vegetables.
Someone who hacks.
One who is inexperienced or unskilled at a particular activity, especially a sport such as golf or tennis.

definitions.definition: One who operates a taxicab
Capacity of mind, especially to understand principles, truths, facts or meanings, acquire knowledge, and apply it to practice; the ability to comprehend and learn.

Information, usually secret, about the enemy or about hostile activities.
A political or military department, agency or unit designed to gather information, usually secret, about the enemy or about hostile activities.

definitions.definition: A Chinese martial art.

A fugitive from the law.

A person who operates outside established norms.

definitions.definition: A wild horse.
An in-law: a relative by marriage.
One who would be an in-law except that the marriage-like relationship is unofficial.
A prostitute who works alone, without a pimp.
To declare illegal.
To place a ban upon.
To remove from legal jurisdiction or enforcement.

definitions.definition: To deprive of legal force.
definitions.example: Laws outlawed by necessity. — Fuller.

definitions.definition: A sweater that must be put on by pulling it over the head; a sweater without buttons or a zipper in front
An exercise performed lying on the back in which the arms are extended behind the head and exertion lifts the weight above the head.
(horizontal bar) An exercise in which the gymnast pulls up from a hang lifting the legs up and over the bar thus rolling into a support position.
An instance of a vehicle being pulled over.
Calm and quiet; peaceful; tranquil

definitions.definition: A footpath or other path or track.

Slightly drunk or intoxicated; tipsy
Crooked, askew; awry

A fetter-like band worn as an ornament.
A link for connecting railroad cars; a drawlink or draglink.
A length of cable or chain equal to 12 1/2 fathoms or 75 feet, or later to 15 fathoms.

definitions.definition: The integral of force over time.

definitions.definition: To impel; to incite.
Not fair, just or right.

definitions.definition: To grasp a concept fully and thoroughly, especially (of words, statements, art, etc.) to be aware of the meaning of and (of people) to be aware of the intent of.

definitions.definition: To believe, to think one grasps sufficiently despite potentially incomplete knowledge.

definitions.definition: (obsolete outside circus, acrobatics) To stand underneath, to support.

synonyms: pick up what someone is putting down

definitions.definition: Violent, destructive and cruel.
Savage and aggressive.
Pertaining to vice; characterised by immorality or depravity.
As seen on a screen (as of television, film, or computer) rather than in real life.

definitions.definition: Inside the frame of the cinema, television or computer screen.

The identification and categorisation of processes or steps that constitute a complex task or mindset in order to render explicit the tacit and implicit.
To join again; to unite after separation.
To come, or go, again into the presence of; to join the company of again.
To state in reply; -- followed by an object clause.

To answer, as the defendant to the plaintiff's replication.

In US patent law To re-insert a patent claim, typically after allowance of a patent application, applied to patent claims that had been withdrawn from examination under a restriction requirement, based on rejoinder .
To arrange randomly; to make random
definitions.example: He randomised the marbles by spilling them onto the floor.

definitions.definition: To imply or require.

To cut or carve in an ornamental way.
The direction or area lying to the east.

definitions.definition: Willing to give and share unsparingly; showing a readiness to give more (especially money) than is expected or needed.

definitions.definition: Large, more than ample, copious.

definitions.definition: Invigorating in its nature.

definitions.definition: Of noble birth.
A person or object meant to lure somebody into danger.
A real or fake animal used by hunters to lure game.

To be formed into a kink or twist.
Bent or twisted into a tight curl.

Neat and tidy; especially used of hair
Large cannon-like weapons, transportable and usually operated by more than one person.
An army unit that uses such weapons.
Gunnery.
A military formation using projectile weapons, such as archers.
A fugitive from the law.
(history) A criminal who is excluded from normal legal rights; one who can be killed at will without legal penalty.
A person who operates outside established norms.

A prostitute who works alone, without a pimp.

definitions.example: Laws outlawed by necessity. — Fuller.

definitions.definition: An amusing story.
Something said or done for amusement, not in seriousness.
definitions.example: It was a joke!

definitions.definition: The root cause or main issue, especially an unexpected one
A laughably worthless thing or person; a sham.
definitions.example: The president was a joke.

definitions.definition: To do or say something for amusement rather than seriously.
definitions.example: I didn’t mean what I said — I was only joking.

definitions.definition: (intransitive, followed by with) To dupe in a friendly manner for amusement; to mess with, play with.
definitions.example: Relax, man, I'm just joking with you.

definitions.definition: To make merry with; to make jokes upon; to rally.
definitions.example: to joke a comrade

definitions.definition: The action of entering, or going in.
definitions.example: Her entrance attracted no attention whatsoever.

definitions.definition: The act of taking possession, as of property, or of office.
definitions.example: the entrance of an heir upon his inheritance, or of a magistrate into office

definitions.definition: The place of entering, as a gate or doorway.
definitions.example: Place your bag by the entrance so that you can find it easily.

definitions.definition: The right to go in.
definitions.example: You'll need a ticket to gain entrance to the museum.

definitions.definition: The entering upon; the beginning, or that with which the beginning is made; the commencement; initiation.
definitions.example: a difficult entrance into business

definitions.definition: The causing to be entered upon a register, as a ship or goods, at a customhouse; an entering.
definitions.example: His entrance of the arrival was made the same day.

definitions.definition: The angle which the bow of a vessel makes with the water at the water line.
The bow, or entire wedgelike forepart of a vessel, below the water line.
When a musician starts playing or singing, entry.
The series of vertebrae, separated by disks, that encloses and protects the spinal cord, and runs down the middle of the back in vertebrate animals.
Any fundamental support, structure, or infrastructure.
definitions.example: Before automobiles, railroads were a backbone of commerce.

definitions.definition: Courage, fortitude, or strength.
definitions.example: He would make a good manager, if he had a little more backbone.

definitions.definition: The quality or state of being gigantic; being of abnormally large size.

A condition where there is over-production of growth hormone by the pituitary gland in a child before the bone growth plates close, resulting in excessive long bone growth, accompanied by muscular weakness and sexual impotence.
A relatively low-gravity beer, often with a dark colour; mild ale
Gentle and not easily angered.

definitions.definition: (of a rule or punishment) Of only moderate severity; not strict.
definitions.example: He received a mild sentence.

definitions.definition: Not overly felt or seriously intended.
(of an illness or pain) Not serious or dangerous.
(of weather) Moderately warm, especially less cold than expected.
definitions.example: a mild day

definitions.definition: (of a medicine or cosmetic) Acting gently and without causing harm.
definitions.example: a mild anaesthetic

definitions.definition: (of food, drink, or a drug) Not sharp or bitter; not strong in flavor.
definitions.example: a mild curry

definitions.definition: An inclination in a particular direction.
definitions.example: the trend of a coastline

definitions.definition: A tendency.
definitions.example: There is a trend, these days, for people in films not to smoke.

definitions.definition: A fad or fashion style.
definitions.example: Miniskirts were one of the biggest trends of the 1960s.

definitions.definition: A line drawn on a graph that approximates the trend of a number of disparate points.
The lower end of the shank of an anchor, being the same distance on the shank from the throat that the arm measures from the throat to the bill.
The angle made by the line of a vessel's keel and the direction of the anchor cable, when she is swinging at anchor.
To have a particular direction; to run; to stretch; to tend.
definitions.example: The shore of the sea trends to the southwest.

definitions.definition: To cause to turn; to bend.
To be the subject of a trend; to be currently popular, relevant or interesting.
definitions.example: What topics have been trending on social networks this week?

definitions.definition: A musical instrument consisting of a flexible metal or bamboo “tongue” attached to a frame. This tongue is placed in the performer’s mouth and plucked with the finger to produce a note of constant pitch. Melodies can be created by changing the shape of the mouth and causing different overtones.
The suit, in a game of cards, that outranks all others.
definitions.example: Diamonds were declared trump(s).

definitions.definition: A playing card of that suit.
definitions.example: He played an even higher trump.

definitions.definition: Something that gives one an advantage, especially one held in reserve.

An old card game, almost identical to whist; the game of ruff.
A card of the major arcana of the tarot.

To impose unfairly; to palm off.
To supersede.

definitions.definition: To outweigh; be stronger, greater, bigger than or in other way superior to.
A trumpet.
Flatulence.
The noise made by an elephant through its trunk.
To blow a trumpet.
To flatulate.
definitions.example: And without warning me, as he lay there, he suddenly trumped next to me in bed.

definitions.definition: The layout of a publication or document.
(hence) The form of presentation of something.
The type of programming that a radio station broadcasts; such as a certain genre of music, news, sports, talk, etc.
definitions.example: The radio station changed the format of its evening program.

definitions.definition: A file type.
To create or edit the layout of a document.
Change a document so it will fit onto a different type of page.
To prepare a mass storage medium for initial use, erasing any existing data in the process.

definitions.definition: Adept at taking or grasping

definitions.definition: Quick and light in movement or action.
definitions.example: He was too nimble for the assailant and easily escaped his grasp.

definitions.definition: Quick-witted and alert.

definitions.definition: A daughter of one’s sibling, brother-in-law, or sister-in-law; either the daughter of one's brother ("fraternal niece"), or of one's sister ("sororal niece").

Mock, pretended.

definitions.definition: To be troubled; to give way to mental anxiety or doubt.

Having a bad smell.

The vertical distance from the ground to the highest part of a standing person or animal (withers in the case of a horse).

An area of land at the top of a cliff.
To instruct, train (usually in matters of knowledge).

To impart information or knowledge.
To act as an informer; denounce.
To give form or character to; to inspire (with a given quality); to affect, influence (with a pervading principle, idea etc.).

definitions.definition: To make known, wisely and/or knowledgeably.

To take form; to become visible or manifest; to appear.
Instructed; having knowledge of a fact or area of education.
definitions.example: An informed young man delivered a lecture on the history of modern art.

definitions.definition: Based on knowledge; founded on due understanding of a situation.
Full of danger.
definitions.example: Railway crossings without gates are highly dangerous.

definitions.definition: Causing danger; ready to do harm or injury.
In a condition of danger, as from illness; threatened with death.
Hard to suit; difficult to please.
Reserved; not affable.
An extremely dangerous location, structure or device
definitions.example: Dude, your car is a deathtrap! Get the brakes and the steering fixed.

definitions.definition: A plot device where a villain in a story puts a hero in an elaborate dangerous situation for the purposes of attempting a sadistic murder
Cause to become one; make into a unit; consolidate; merge; combine.
Become one.
Eager or inordinate desire for some object that confers distinction, as preferment, honor, superiority, political power, or literary fame; desire to distinguish one's self from other people.
definitions.example: My son, John, wants to be a firefighter very much. He has a lot of ambition.

definitions.definition: An object of an ardent desire.
definitions.example: My ambition is to own a helicopter.

definitions.definition: A desire, as in (sense 1), for another person to achieve these things.
A personal quality similar to motivation, not necessarily tied to a single goal.
The act of going about to solicit or obtain an office, or any other object of desire; canvassing.
To seek after ambitiously or eagerly; to covet.
definitions.example: Pausanias, ambitioning the sovereignty of Greece, bargains with Xerxes for his daughter in marriage. — Trumbull.
